name: Azlund
epithet: of the House of Healing, whose oath bound the road

name: Tessa
epithet: who walked the company down to the docks where the Horizon was moored

name: Rindal
epithet: first mate of the Horizon, whose tour named the crew

name: Captain Brennan
epithet: veteran of the Nyanzaru run, who gave them a cloak of billowing for six ropes held

name: Pip
epithet: cook of the Horizon, who minded the cheese and would not be lied to

name: Corwin, called Artus Cimber
epithet: who hid his name in a glove and a ring, and turned alone to the pirates so the company might live

name: Dragonbait
epithet: saurial of many scents, who held the prow when the sahuagin came

name: Harlen
epithet: Zhentarim spy, the wrong sailor, the cheese-thief, knocked cold in the galley as the party was teleported away

name: The Fleeing Yuan-ti
epithet: who struck from the rooftops and fled before the reckoning

name: Torgg
epithet: officer of the ship's crew who dismissed a crewmate for incompetence and yielded the hiring manifest when Anton asked

name: Volo
epithet: the wandering chronicler met near the Temple of Savras, who sold the company his Guide to Monsters and bade them find a guide before the jungle

name: Lilac Mist
epithet: tabaxi dinosaur racer of Port Nyanzaru, whose wayward triceratops first brought her to the company's attention, and who finished the track ahead of them all

name: Kessra
epithet: nearly lost overboard when the storm struck, caught at the rail by Vex with Anton bracing him

name: Wakanga
epithet: one of Port Nyanzaru's seven merchant princes, who gave Urida a journal pointing to a magical object in the jungle and asked her to retrieve it

name: Musharib
epithet: a guide in Jobal's employ, unimpressed by the company's means and motives, who will take them to Hrakhamar at his asking price

name: Zindar
epithet: gold half-dragon harbormaster, who bid the company safe passage and named the pirates a challenge not yet answered

name: Inete
epithet: who paid a hundred gold to follow her visions of a red-robed wizard into the Aldani Basin

name: Undril Silvertusk
epithet: who sought the company's escort to Camp Vengeance, her payment promised only on arrival

name: Grandfather Zitembe
epithet: of the Temple of Savras in Port Nyanzaru, whose guides were consulted for a donation and who came when his acolyte had fallen

name: Ekene-Afa
epithet: merchant prince of Port Nyanzaru whose commerce is weapons, and who recognized Lilac by face from the racing circuit

name: Odele
epithet: a name spoken in concern, warning the captain of a storm in the ship's path

name: Eku
epithet: a guide for hire, whose name appeared on the bulletin boards of Port Nyanzaru alongside Musharib and Salida

name: Salida
epithet: a jungle guide whose name appeared on the bulletin boards of Port Nyanzaru, known to work for Jobal

name: Azaka Stormfang
epithet: whose name appeared on a wanted poster outside the Thundering Lizard Inn, alongside Hew Hackinstone

name: Hew Hackinstone
epithet: a wanted face whose poster hung beside Azaka Stormfang's on the board outside the Thundering Lizard Inn

name: Jobal
epithet: merchant prince of Port Nyanzaru, who controls the jungle guides

name: Saja N'baza
epithet: a contact in Chult whom Artus Cimber was traveling to meet in hopes of finding his lost wife

name: Jessamine
epithet: one of the seven merchant princes of Port Nyanzaru, suspected by Grandfather Zitembe of dealing in underhanded affairs

name: Kwayothe
epithet: merchant prince of Port Nyanzaru, whose trade runs in tej and insect repellant

name: Ifan Talro'a
epithet: merchant prince of Port Nyanzaru who controls the sale of animals

name: Zhanthi
epithet: one of Port Nyanzaru's merchant princes, dealer in gems and jewelry

name: Yokka
epithet: goblin boss of Camp Righteous who surrendered after her last minion fell, then bartered information for her freedom

name: Niles Breakbone
epithet: commandant of Camp Vengeance, whose mood is foul and whose reinforcements never came

name: Ord Firebeard
epithet: a soldier of Camp Vengeance, who spoke plainly of the camp's long deterioration

name: Perne Salhana
epithet: soldier of Camp Vengeance, who spoke with Vex of a camp falling apart

name: Wulf Rygor
epithet: a scout at Camp Vengeance, in whose tent a strangely enchanted plant was found after his return from the field
